Episode 497: Know Your Verb, Get Better Results

March 8, 2017 by Ivan Misner 18 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Synopsis

Dr. Misner recently attended a workshop by Alex Mandossian, who gave a workshop at the Transformational Leadership Council conference called “Discover Your Verb.” In this talk, Alex said that the one thing that distinguishes a great leader is movement.

Verbs increase your persuasion power. You’ll get more business if you use more verbs and fewer adverbs and adjectives. If you look at great thought leaders throughout history, they all have a characteristic verb.

  • Rene Descartes: Think
  • Maria Montessori: Teach
  • Walt Disney: Dream
  • Ivan Misner: Inspire

What’s your verb? What action characterizes you, not just in your business but in all aspects of your life? Leave your verb, and your reason for choosing it, in the comments.

Brought to you by the Networking for Success YouTube Channel.

Complete Transcript of Episode 497 –

Priscilla:
Hello everybody and welcome back to the Official BNI Podcast, brought to you by the Networking for Success Channel on YouTube, featuring Dr. Ivan Miser and many other networking experts. I am Priscilla Rice, and I am coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, California. I am joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan. How are you and where are you today?

Ivan:
Hey. I am actually in Austin all month this month. It’s a miracle. I am in one place for a few weeks.

Priscilla:
That’s great. Can you explain this topic, Ivan?

Ivan:
Yes. It’s an unusual topic. What does knowing your verb have to do with getting more business? A month ago, I spent five days at my semi annual TLC – Transformational Leadership Council – event. It was in Mexico. I always come away with a lot from these conferences. I come away with some great nuggets of information.

TLC was started by Jack Canfield, who has become a really good friend. He is the co-author of Chicken Soup for the Soul. At this conference, one of the presentations that gave me a lot of good nuggets was from another good friend of mine who is a member, Alex Mandossian. In his talk, he did a talk called, “Discover Your Verb”.

I thought it sounded little weird but his content is always so great. So I had to be there, and I am really glad I was. It was, in fact, quite amazing. He said, “The biggest lesson that I have learned over the years is the one thing that makes a great business person and a leader is movement, taking action. Albert Einstein once said that nothing happens until something moves. This is true in leadership and it is true in business because without movement change is not possible.”

Alex told the story of the legendary ad man, Leo Burnett from Chicago. He said that Burnett once put his staff to the task of analyzing 62 ads that failed to moved merchandise. He wanted to know why they failed so he asked for ideas on why they failed. In looking at them, the thought the they had too many adjectives like “extremely” or “very” because adjectives don’t move people. Instead, they if anything can spark skepticism in people’s minds.

It turns out that in fact, those ads- of the 12,758 words in the ads that they looked at – the 62 ads – 24.1% of them were adjectives. As many as 24.1% of the 12,000 words were adjectives like “extremely” and “very”. Alex said that in comparison, Lincoln’s Gettysburg Address contains only 13% adjectives in the total word ration. Churchill’s “Blood, Sweat and Tears” speech had only 12% adjectives to word speech.

So if adjectives are the problem, he suggested or asked what is the solution?He suggested that it wasn’t nouns. It was about verbs. Action words. Did verbs increase persuasion power and move people? The greatest thought leaders in history led their lives as verbs. For example, Rene Descartes, father of western philosophy. His verb was “think”. Coggito ergo sum. I think therefore I am.

Einstein believed if you stopped learning, you started dying. His verb was “learn”. I learn therefore I am.

Maria Montessori believed in the teaching philosophy that bears her name today. Her verb was “teach”. I teach therefore I am.

Walt Disney believed in dreaming. His verb won him 22 Academy Awards. I dream therefore I am.

It is a one word language that moves people and causes permanent and positive change. So my question to my listeners on the podcast is what is your verb?

That day, Alex asked us to pick our verb and put it on a sticky note. Originally, I chose “collaborate” because I thought that was what BNI was all about, collaboration. But my wife, Beth, looked at me and said, “That is not your verb.”

I said, “What? What do you mean that is not my verb? My business is all about collaboration.”

She said, “Yeah. That is how you operate, but that is not the big picture of what you do.”

I said, “Okay. What do you mean?”

She said, “I think you inspire.'”

Alex told us that your verb had to be something big. It has to be the big movement that we have with the people that we work with. Beth said that she felt that my role is to inspire people to collaborate. And I think she was right.

Priscilla:
Yeah, that sounds good.

Ivan:
My verb is “inspire” and I view that as different than “motivate” because I honestly don’t believe that I can motivate people. I think only the individual can motivate themselves. But I might be able to give some type of inspiration through my teaching, through my speaking that motivates people to act. So my verb, Priscilla, is “inspire”.

I have to ask you, what do you think your verb is? Think big.

Priscilla:
I did a little bit of thinking about this because you asked me this in advance and what I could come up with is I like to help people and I like to connect people to various things, all kinds of things. So those were the two verbs that I came up with but I am not sure that either of them are on the grand scheme of things what you are talking about.

Ivan:
I think it could be absolutely be “help”. That may, in fact, be it.

What I would like my listeners to do is to think about what their verb is. If you are listening to this podcast, think about it. What do you do? Think as big as you can. What is it that you do in your business that is an action word that gets people to understand the big picture of what you do in your business?

I would even suggest that your chapter one week – you want to do this in advance. Don’t spring it on the chapter. Give them a week’s notice. Ask everyone to listen to this podcast and then come to the meeting. That week, share your action word, your verb. It has to be one word. You have however much time you have in the chapters. Some chapters have 30 seconds, others have a minute. You have however much time you have and in the time you have left, explain why.

I think that is a powerful weekly presentation, especially if everyone did it and shared their very and then explained why to people. Even what I just did with “inspire”. My job is to inspire people from what I have learned and what I teach to go out and be the most successful businessperson they possibly can, particularly through referrals through networking.

So that is my presentation for today, Priscilla. I would love to hear the listeners. Please, comment on this podcast. Put your verb and why it is your verb. And if you do this at your chapter, come back to this podcast and let us know how it all went. That is all I have for today, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Okay, that’s great. That’s very interesting. I have to do some more thinking about it. Thank you, Ivan. I really appreciate it. I would just like to remind the listeners that this podcast has been brought to you by the Networking for Success Channel on YouTube. Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of the Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Posted in Networking Tagged with Alex Mandossian

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Jan Wilson says

    March 28, 2017 at 2:42 pm

    My verb is embrace because 1) it is a verb and a noun. As a verb it means to hold many time with affection, to accept and support. As a noun it is the act of holding many times with affection as well as the act of accepting and supporting. I really liked this podcast and as the Education Coordinator of my chapter asked that the chapter listen to and be prepared to share their verb at our next meeting. Thank you

    Reply

  2. Marcia Macomber says

    April 1, 2017 at 11:17 am

    My verb is create (goes with my business name: Cornucopia Creations)! I create opportunities for my clients to connect with prospective and existing clients. I create marketing strategies to grow their businesses; I create graphics, websites and copy to more clearly convey clients’ unique selling propositions to customers.

    Loved this concept. I took it to our chapter (The Business Building Network, Sonoma, CA). In many cases, members listened to their first-ever podcast! They came back last week and presented their verb during the weekly presentation. This provided everyone with a new perspective on each member and their business. This is excellent to add to the growing list of meeting stimulants. Thanks.

    Reply

  3. Deborah Javier says

    April 1, 2017 at 1:39 pm

    I have revisited this podcast several times. It encouraged me to look at myself and access what is my Verb.
    My verb would be Create – I am an Interior Designer and nothing gives me more pleasure than taking a space, a conversation, or several and turning those ideas into a fulfilling and pleasurable space that reflects and even evokes the very best in the user.

    Reply

  4. Ross Davis says

    April 5, 2017 at 7:19 pm

    WOW! This BNI Podcast was a home-run in my chapter. As an Educational Coordinator for my chapter, I sometimes wonder if my 3-5 minute presentations die in the meeting or do they follow my members out the door after the meeting. Well that question was laid to rest last month. After doing a educational training moment about this episode, I challenged my members to to do a training moment the following week and share their verb with everyone and why they chose that verb. Of the 39 members who were in attendance that following week, 33 of them completely rewrote their training moments around their action verb. We had everything from help, to dig, to “quack” and well we even had a few adjectives but I gave them an “E” for effort. We even had a few members continue the action verb theme the following week. It was so gratifying to see that not only were my BNI members listening, they were inspired enough to take action. Thank you for this great podcast! For the record, my action verb was HELP which is also part of my license plate.

    Reply

  5. Bonnie J Sullivan, PhD says

    April 10, 2017 at 2:23 pm

    Mine is Love. Though I take many actions in my work as a psychologist, Love is the active ingredient.
    Thank you for the podcast; I will use it in my next infomercial!

    Reply

  6. George Barnett says

    April 21, 2017 at 1:59 pm

    We used this great meeting stimulant at the Trussville Chapter of BNI. We announced to members before an upcoming holiday week about doing a CEU Challenge. This gave members 2 weeks to listen to the podcast and really put thought into what their Verb will be.

    The results were outstanding. Members and guests heard many well thought out presentations. Our chapter members verbs. We also did a word cloud for chapter Facebook page. Design, Protect, Sale, Listen, Inform, Grow, Teach, Educate, Comfort, Discover, Serve, Tell, Pamper, Beautify, Specialize, Trust, Restore, Respond, Resolve, Strip, Write, Help, Create, Ease, Provide, Assure, Transform

    My verb is Discover.
    Our chapter’s verb is Transform

    Reply

    • kevin says

      January 5, 2020 at 2:45 pm

      I love it.
      A word cloud. great idea
      A chapter Verb. great idea.

      Reply

  7. Piotr Grz?dziel says

    May 24, 2017 at 5:09 am

    Hi,

    this is a great thing! Thank you Ivan & thank you Alex. Will introduce this idea tomorrow during the meeting (Warsaw / Poland(, do the follow up next week and let you know later how it went.

    Truly inspiring

    Ave!

    Reply

  8. Sonja Sutton says

    May 30, 2017 at 10:17 am

    Good afternoon, my name is Sonja Sutton with Colonial Benefits. Now, I don’t know if it’s because today is my first day out of the house after being home with pneumonia for 12 days, but the verb I came up with for myself is INFECT. Not in that horrible way, but my goal, every day, is to INFECT those I come in contact with. I want to INFECT them with my attitude, my empathy, my positive-ness, my energy, my passion, and my compassion. My goal is for people to want to be around me because I make them feel better about themselves. I believe having a positive attitude and INFECTING others with it will make this world a better place. And if more of us become INFECTED with POSITIVE, and go out and share that positive, then we truly WILL make a difference.

    Reply

    • Sabrina Khamisani says

      May 31, 2017 at 12:58 am

      well said Sonja,i agree with you.

      Reply

  9. Dr Andrew Chiew says

    August 1, 2017 at 8:41 am

    Fantastic learning material!
    My key to success has always been to be DIFFERENT to Make a DIFFERENCE.
    Therefore, I think my ‘Verb’ will be ‘Challenge’, ie to challenge the status quo.
    I CHALLENGE therefore I am.

    Reply

  10. Carolyn McLain says

    January 10, 2018 at 1:35 pm

    I believe my verb is educate. I say this as I always want everyone to know what I know. And if they don’t, I want to educate them so they do. In my business and in my life I am always educating people.

    Reply

  11. Jared Quartell says

    October 21, 2019 at 7:44 am

    Loved this episode!! Targeted a key topic that is practical and useful in so many ways. Thank you Dr Ivan Misner.

    Reply

  12. kevin says

    January 5, 2020 at 3:25 pm

    I’m having a lot of trouble with selecting just a single word. I run a training and coaching business, so I could pick ‘TRAIN’ or ‘COACH’ and it would be kinda right. Or maybe ‘TEACH’, ‘GUIDE’, or ‘ADVISE’ which are somewhere in between. Also pretty good choices. My company’s name is Sharpen IT, so another good choice is ‘SHARPEN’. I sharpen skills. I sharpen focus. I also resonate with ‘ACHIEVE’, ‘ACCOMPLISH’, ‘GAIN’, ‘PROCESS’, ‘ENCOURAGE’, ‘MOTIVATE’, and ‘PUSH’. I could create my own word cloud. :-)

    But after a lot of soul searching, I’m going to go with ‘DRIVE’. I feel it is a very strong verb and describes both how I DRIVE myself, and how I DRIVE and my clients to achieve results from their team and their business that gives them the freedom of more business and more time. Sometimes I’m in the Driver’s seat, leading the way, and at other times, I’m the Driving motivation supporting from behind. And I also enjoy the times when I’m the co-pilot as we Drive towards our goals together.

    Reply

  13. Leanne Watson says

    January 5, 2020 at 11:10 pm

    I too enjoyed this podcast.

    My word is Transform and it’s already in the material I use in talking about my business :)

    Reply

  14. Ernie Garcia says

    January 10, 2020 at 6:12 am

    Great, I am prepared to revolutionize!

    Reply

  15. Colin Horner says

    January 26, 2021 at 7:40 pm

    An amazing re-visit to this Podcast this morning having evolved my own Project 2021 over the last couple of months My Verb “Explain” and Project 2021: to XPlain “how your BNI Connect Profile can become a referral generating machine” You have inspired me to keep trying…

    Reply

  16. Colin Horner says

    February 2, 2021 at 1:29 am

    Hi Ivan on a re-listen to this Podcast and with my current Project 2021 “Using BNI Technology” I believe Find your Verb has far greater significance for the whole strategy of your business mission. Using this as the key to opening up your Vision Board. I base one of my latest Edu Moments on #497: https://youtu.be/MM-rz9a5e5E
    kind regards & #BNIThankyou
    Colin
    #IXPlain

    Reply
